ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn code exists for bacon and berry jam pizza specifically; closest parent is the general pizza food product class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers pizza as a category; no specific code exists for this topping combination.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for this specific pizza variant; category-level pizza codes are not sufficiently specific.

serving.natural_language_portion: One whole individual pizza of approximately 300 g, as typically served at a restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Pizza, cheese, from frozen, baked — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; supplemented with USDA entries for smoked bacon (NDB 10124) and berry jam (NDB 19297) for topping adjustment
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct USDA or HPB entry exists for this specific restaurant pizza. Values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for cheese pizza base (FDC ID 170919) with proportional adjustments for smoked bacon, sweet corn, berry sauce, and grana padano toppings. The base pizza contributes approximately 70% of the mass; toppings contribute 30%. Energy and macros are weighted composites. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Pizza, cheese, thin crust (GI ~50, glucose reference).
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific bacon and berry jam pizza. GI of 50 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for thin-crust cheese pizza (GI ~50, glucose scale), which is the closest analogous food. The berry jam topping adds simple sugars but the overall GI is moderated by the cheese and protein content. GL per serving calculated as (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (50 x 85.5) / 100 = 42.8, where carbohydrate per 300 g serving = 28.5 x 3 = 85.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from smoked bacon and aged grana padano cheese
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this pizza due to its estimated sodium content of approximately 580 mg per 100 g (approximately 1740 mg per full serving), which represents a substantial portion of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (GI 50) combined with high glycemic load per serving due to large carbohydrate portion
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with portion size as the glycemic load per full serving is approximately 42.8, which is high; consuming a half-portion with a side salad and monitoring postprandial glucose is advisable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from bacon, mozzarella, and grana padano, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit consumption of this pizza due to its saturated fat content (approximately 4.1 g per 100 g) and high sodium load from cured meats and aged cheese, both of which are associated with adverse cardiovascular outcomes when consumed in excess.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bacon and Berry Jam Pizza is an Italian-style artisan pizza served at Mingle restaurant in Funan, Singapore. It features a pizza base topped with fior di latte (fresh cow's milk mozzarella), smoked bacon, sweet corn kernels, a berry sauce or jam, and grana padano (a hard Italian aged cheese similar to Parmigiano-Reggiano). The combination of savoury smoked bacon and sweet berry sauce creates a sweet-savoury flavour profile that is characteristic of contemporary Italian-inspired restaurant menus. The fior di latte provides a creamy, milky texture, while the grana padano adds a sharp, nutty finish. The pizza is baked in a deck or wood-fired oven, producing a crisp base with a soft, airy interior. Nutritionally, this pizza is a moderate-to-high calorie dish estimated at approximately 242 kcal per 100 g, with meaningful protein from the cheese and bacon, and a notable sodium content due to the cured meat and aged cheese. The glycemic index is estimated at 50 (low), but the glycemic load per full serving is high due to the large carbohydrate portion from the pizza base. Clinically, this dish is of concern for individuals managing hypertension due to its high sodium content, and for those with cardiovascular disease due to its saturated fat load. People with Type 2 Diabetes should manage portion size carefully given the high per-serving glycemic load.
common_user_queries: What is Bacon and Jam pizza?
How many calories in a bacon pizza?
What is fior di latte on pizza?
Is this pizza suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry exists in any approved primary source for this specific restaurant pizza. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for cheese pizza base with proportional topping adjustments. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (thin-crust cheese pizza, GI 50) from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level USDA data is high quality and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy cheese pizza entry and cannot be reliably estimated for this composite dish. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA source for this food category. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry used as the base. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin C, vitamin A, B vitamins, folate, phosphorus, magnesium, and zinc are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and are approximate. The food_name has been normalised from the input menu name 'Bacon and Jam' to the more descriptive canonical form 'Bacon and Berry Jam Pizza' to accurately reflect the dish type and ingredients. The food_group 'Grains and staples' is preserved from the input as pizza is grain-based. This record covers the dish as served at Mingle - Funan, Singapore, sourced from GrabFood Singapore (GD004132). Price SGD 32.50. As this is an Italian cuisine dish (not a Singaporean dish), multilingual naming requirements for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi do not apply; only the romanized Italian name is provided as a local-language reference. The alias 'Pizza con Pancetta e Marmellata di Frutti di Bosco' was added to the flat aliases list to ensure consistency with aliases_by_language.
